summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for Homebrew distribution, including bundling the server inside the app and setting up automated GitHub Actions CI to build and publish DMG releases.
summary.outcome: CI pipeline was set up with electron-builder, the server was bundled inside the Electron app via extraResources, a Homebrew tap repo was initialized, and the workflow was iterated to fix multiple CI failures. The tag-based release trigger was restored at the end.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: root (server/gossamer-serve) and app/ (Electron frontend) — they were intentionally separated but are now tightly coupled since the server is bundled inside the app.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT and find Prisma binaries, so the bundled layout must preserve relative path structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema — it must be in production dependencies.
utilityProcess.fork() requires a CJS entry point; the server is ESM, requiring a CJS wrapper (server-launcher.cjs) that dynamically imports the ESM server.
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) must be rebuilt against Electron's ABI using @electron/rebuild in CI before packaging.
The Electron app detects dev vs prod via presence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL to gate whether the main process starts the bundled server.

code.finding: Modified to spawn gossamer-serve as a utilityProcess in production using the bundled server-launcher.cjs
New CJS wrapper created to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only) to the ESM server via dynamic import()
Added electron-builder, extraResources config pointing to ../dist, ../node_modules, ../prisma, and a repository field required by electron-builder for publish detection

Workflow installs root deps, runs @electron/rebuild for native modules, prunes devDeps, then builds with --publish never; softprops/action-gh-release handles DMG upload; a separate step auto-updates the Homebrew cask formula with real SHA256s
Cask template stored in main repo; copied to ../homebrew-gossamer/Casks/gossamer.rb for the tap repo
prisma moved from devDependencies to dependencies so npm prune --production retains it

workflow: electron-builder auto-detects the GitHub repo from .git/config — this fails when package.json is in a subdirectory; must explicitly set the repository field in app/package.json.
electron-builder's default publish behavior skips uploading when not on a tag; use --publish never and let a separate GitHub Actions step (softprops/action-gh-release) handle artifact upload.
Homebrew tap repos must follow the homebrew-<name> naming convention with cask files in a Casks/ subdirectory.
Fine-grained GitHub PATs for pushing to a tap repo only need Contents (read/write) and Metadata (read) permissions scoped to the tap repository.
When app/package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json, npm ci fails — must run npm install locally and commit the updated lock file.
Pushing commits to a non-checked-out branch can be done via a temporary git worktree.

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync with package.json after adding electron-builder, causing CI to fail with EUSAGE on npm ci.
electron-builder couldn't detect the GitHub repo because .git is in the parent directory of app/, requiring an explicit repository field.
electron-builder tried to publish DMGs itself instead of deferring to softprops; required adding --publish never flag.
Network outage prevented pushing commits for an extended period, leaving several changes in a locally-committed but unpushed state.
The worktree approach for committing to the README branch without checkout picked up the local branch tip instead of origin/README, causing confusion about commit ordering.
CI trigger was temporarily changed to branch pushes for testing, requiring a manual revert to tag-based trigger at the end of the session.
open_items: App is unsigned — users must bypass macOS Gatekeeper via System Settings on first launch. Long-term fix requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and signing secrets in CI.
The app/ and root directory separation is acknowledged as awkward now that the server is bundled, but consolidation was explicitly deferred.
HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret must be added to gossamer repo settings before a real tag-based release can auto-update the cask formula.
The tag-based release workflow has not yet been end-to-end tested with an actual v* tag push.
The cask formula still has placeholder SHA256 values that will only be filled in correctly once the first real CI-triggered release runs.
